step_by_step_reasoning: Based on the whiteboard image and transcript, there are nine core elements shown. We represent the local TV studio as OnPremDC (incorporating the camera/encoder icon). We then map Systems Manager (which coordinates on-premises automation), the three AWS Elemental services (MediaStore, MediaLive, and MediaPackage), S3 (used for archiving), and the downstream AI/ML services (Rekognition and Transcribe). Lastly, CloudFront serves as the CDN distribution layer.

graph.name: Graham Media Group: Automating and Modernizing Live Broadcast Streaming
graph.notes: Architects a transition from traditional broadcast to a digital solution using AWS Elemental media services, S3 archiving, ML-driven recognition, and Systems Manager for remote on-prem orchestration.

nodes.name: Local TV Encoder
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Captures camera feeds on-premises and pushes stream to the cloud.

nodes.name: Systems Manager
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Remotely triggers local encoder APIs and triggers ad-breaks via agent.

nodes.name: MediaStore
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Contribution stream endpoint and buffer.

nodes.name: MediaPackage
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Formats stream for diverse playback targets (mobile, TV).

nodes.name: CloudFront
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Direct video delivery to end users.

nodes.name: Rekognition
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Identifies faces and video segments for indexing.

nodes.name: Transcribe
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Translates speech to text for automated video captioning.

edges.notes: Archives stream recordings directly to S3.
Processes archived video for facial and object recognition.
Automates on-premises encoders using Systems Manager agent.
